methodology: Articles sourced from Virginia-specific RSS feeds and Google News RSS, filtered for AI/technology/workforce relevance, then assessed by Claude against the scenario framework. Each article is tagged with the region it most affects and the scenario it signals toward.

signals.title: AI pressure grows on Richmond's young workforce - Axios
signals.signal_reading: Young workers facing AI pressure without corresponding education infrastructure signals shallow anxiety rather than deep capability building, concentrating stress without distributing skills.

signals.title: New partnership between VCU and AI Ready RVA aims to boost AI education, innovation and community impact - VCU News
signals.signal_reading: VCU's partnership with AI Ready RVA creates institutional infrastructure for community-scale AI education, potentially distributing deep capability through structured peer networks beyond the university walls.

signals.title: The University of Virginia College and Graduate School of Arts & Sciences and UVA Library Have Founded the AI Literacy and Action Lab | Newswise - Newswise
signals.signal_reading: UVA's AI Literacy and Action Lab signals investment in foundational capability-building rather than just platform adoption, though statewide impact depends on whether the lab's knowledge diffuses beyond Charlottesville.

signals.title: NoVA School Systems Grapple with AI’s Role in the Classroom - Northern Virginia Magazine
signals.signal_reading: NoVA schools grappling with AI in classrooms suggests platform-delivered tools arriving faster than pedagogical depth, risking shallow integration concentrated in wealthy districts with tech budgets.

signals.title: AI is here in Hampton Roads, and businesses and organizations are embracing it - The Virginian-Pilot
signals.signal_reading: Business AI adoption in Hampton Roads indicates emerging capability clusters in the defense/maritime economy, but concentration among organizations with resources rather than distributed workforce fluency.
